title: Adjacent-Rival Reaction Features
summary: The Frontiers F1 paper reports that pitting by the driver ahead materially raises pit probability, and newer multi-agent F1 work models pit timing as opponent-responsive.
rationale: The Frontiers F1 paper reports that pitting by the driver ahead materially raises pit probability, and newer multi-agent F1 work models pit timing as opponent-responsive. This suggests the strongest missing interaction signal is not generic field context but the immediate local battle around a car.
implementation_hint: For each Race x Lap snapshot, engineer only contemporaneous or previous-lap neighbor features: compound/tyre-life of cars at positions +/-1 or +/-2, whether they pitted last lap, local stint-age dispersion, same-team double-stack proxy, and whether an ahead car just opened an undercut/cover window. Optionally train a small neighborhood specialist and rank-average it with the base tree model.
expected_effect: Captures cover-stop and undercut reactions that single-car features miss.
risk: Do not use same-lap future pit outcomes from rivals; neighbor features must be based on current row state or prior laps only.
